full_text:
Instructions for sterilizing at Biomedicum 1 and 2U

If you have any wishes regarding the products, for example, you need certain products to be sterilized, please send a request to our joint email:

valinehuolto-meilahti@helsinki.fi

When you send us a message, please remember to tell us which lab you are working in, which floor your lab is located on (including which house), and your contact information so we can contact you if needed.

Products to be sterilized

The products will be ready at the earliest the next day.

The expiry date of the product is indicated on the products. In the case of two dates, the earlier date is the date of sterilization.

Incubator shelves are sterilized only for old incubators that do not have a decontamination program. (Of course, we wash the shelves of all incubators).

Certain items are automatically sterilized after washing, for example, ultracentrifuge tubes and instruments. These products are marked by equipment maintenance using autoclavable marking tapes.

Do the following:

Email us a service request if you need general glassware sterilized (e.g., Erlenmeyer, etc.). We will bring the product you requested to the nearest service point as soon as possible.

If you wish to have your clean items (e.g., Eppendorf tubes, etc.) back sterilized, bring them in sterilizing bags to the nearest service point.

Fill the bag only up to 60%. Otherwise, steam may not disperse around all products and sterilize them properly. Also, the bag must have space for the seams, i.e., do not cut it too short.

The text should be at the end of the bag, i.e., outside the seams, not in the sterile region.

Be sure to send the tip boxes to be washed occasionally.

Avoid the following:

Putting dirty items that need to be washed among clean things to be sterilized because we must assume all items are contaminated and rewash them all.

Putting a clean product to be sterilized (e.g., sterilization bag) in a dirty washbasin. The product is contaminated, and we must dispose of the bag and its goods in the trash.

To bag a dirty product in an autoclave bag. Please follow the instructions above. We assume that the bagged items are clean and ready for sterilization.

Liquids to be sterilized

Bring all liquids to be sterilized to the service point, where the service personnel will retrieve them.

Always mark on the bottle what the solution is (every bottle). The personnel will not autoclave unknown liquids. (Quality and safety issue). Also, mark who’s it is (Lab name and location, for example, lab A, 2U, 3floor) so that we know where to return it.

Fill bottles full (for example, 1L, not on the narrowing part of the bottle). This applies also to critical concentration solutions, such as 10XPBS.

We do not autoclave 2L bottles with liquids, as they take a very long time in the autoclave.

Please note that autoclaving water does not improve the water quality. The water from the pure water machines is ultrapure—only the cost increases as liquids run in vain with a slower, more water-consuming program.

We autoclave special solutions such as AGARs and DEPC waters on Wednesdays starting before noon. They will be ready in the afternoon. Please bring the solutions to the nearest equipment service point by Tuesday.

Also, bring DEPC waters and DEPC-flushed, bagged products to a service point. They are not picked up by laboratory technicians from fume hoods. (DEPC evaporates CO2 and ethanol in lesser amounts so that they can be safely taken to a service point).

Fill these bottles only up to 70% of the volume because they are autoclaved with a specific program.

Marking the products:

If you use your own printed stickers on the caps, please use dry-heat indicator tape beneath it (v-figure on the tape). Otherwise, the stickers are tough to remove.

When you mark your bottles, do not “go wild” with tape. Please see the examples below.
